name: Camino Vadiniense and San Glorio Loop
name_original: Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa — circular desde Camaleño
description: Cycle the challenging Camino Vadiniense and San Glorio Loop through Picos de Europa National Park, with 8260 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: No traffic touring cycling routes in Lic-Rio Deva traverse a diverse landscape shaped by the Picos de Europa mountains and the Deva River basin. The region features dramatic limestone gorges, lush riverine ecosystems, and varied terrain from mountain passes to coastal estuaries. Cyclists can experience routes along the Deva River, through areas like La Hermida Gorge, and near the Estuary of Tina Mayor. This protected Natura 2000 site offers a well-preserved environment for touring cycling. Best no traffic touring cycling routes in Lic-Rio Deva The most popular no traffic touring cycling route is Access to Pechón – Viewpoint of Sable Beach loop from Cueva el Pindal, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes about 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views towards Sable Beach. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Somaniezo and Cahecho – circular from Potes, a difficult 14.4 miles (23.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the Cantabrian countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Historic center of Potes – Infantado Tower loop from Liébana, a 20.2 miles (32.5 km) trail leading through the historic town of Potes, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Touring cycling in Lic-Rio Deva is defined by mountain passes, river valleys, and dramatic gorges, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Lic-Rio Deva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 370 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lic-Rio Deva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no traffic touring cycling routes are available in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Lic-Rio Deva offers nearly 20 dedicated no traffic touring cycling routes. These routes are carefully curated to provide a serene and enjoyable experience away from vehicular traffic, allowing you to fully immerse yourself in the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly no traffic touring cycling routes in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: While many routes in Lic-Rio Deva are challenging, there are a few moderate options suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40317263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Access to Pechón – Viewpoint of Sable Beach loop from Cueva el Pindal&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that offers beautiful coastal views without extreme difficulty.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on these no traffic touring cycling routes?
A: The no traffic routes in Lic-Rio Deva traverse a diverse range of stunning landscapes. You'll encounter the dramatic topography of the Picos de Europa, lush riverine ecosystems along the Deva River, and the impressive limestone gorge of La Hermida. Expect a mix of challenging mountain passes, picturesque valleys, and even coastal environments near the Estuary of Tina Mayor.

Q: Are there any circular no traffic touring cycling routes in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as circular loops, bringing you back to your starting point. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012455276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somaniezo and Cahecho – circular from Potes&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a rewarding loop through the scenic countryside starting and ending in the charming village of Potes.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see along these routes?
A: Many routes pass by or near significant points of interest. You can explore the historic charm of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/991425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Potes&lt;/a&gt;, visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Lebeña Church&lt;/a&gt;, or marvel at the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4135071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Infantado Tower&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linares to La Hermida Mountain Road&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7402100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Fuente Dé from Potes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: The komoot community highly rates the touring cycling experience in Lic-Rio Deva, with an average score of 3.9 stars. Reviewers often praise the dramatic scenery, the tranquility of the no traffic routes, and the rewarding challenges offered by the Picos de Europa's topography. The well-preserved natural environment and charming villages like Potes are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is Lic-Rio Deva suitable for family-friendly no traffic touring cycling?
A: While many routes are challenging, families with older children or those comfortable with moderate distances and some elevation gain can find suitable options. The focus on 'no traffic' ensures a safer and more relaxed environment for family rides. Look for routes described as 'moderate' in difficulty for a more family-friendly experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Lic-Rio Deva?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Lic-Rio Deva is generally from spring to early autumn (April to October). During these months, the weather is typically milder and more stable, making for more pleasant riding conditions. Summer offers warmer temperatures, while spring and autumn provide vibrant foliage and fewer crowds. Be aware that mountain passes can still be chilly or have unpredictable weather outside of peak summer.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of historical sites and natural beauty?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in both. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Potes – Infantado Tower loop from Liébana&lt;/a&gt; combines cultural exploration of Potes with scenic views of the surrounding natural landscape. You'll find many routes that weave through charming villages and past historical structures while showcasing the stunning environment.

Q: Where can I find parking for these no traffic touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes start from towns and villages like Potes, which typically offer public parking facilities. For routes venturing into more remote areas, it's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information. Parking availability can vary, especially during peak season.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant elevation gains for advanced cyclists?
A: Yes, Lic-Rio Deva, bordering the Picos de Europa, is renowned for its challenging terrain. Many of the no traffic touring routes feature significant elevation gains, making them ideal for advanced cyclists seeking a demanding ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20499522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Oviedo Trail – Potes Old Town loop from Liébana&lt;/a&gt;, for example, involves substantial climbing, offering a true test of endurance and rewarding panoramic views.
tips: From the Fuente de los Asturianos, going down from Áliba to Mogrovejo, a very fun track to go down, with a very good road surface for the most part, some steep slopes where you have to go down with caution. Beautiful views and beautiful forest.
Hard ramps (17-21%). Variable terrain but in good condition. There is a single source of water at the beginning of the track

If there is an impressive and easily accessible viewpoint, the Collado de Llesba is undoubtedly one of the best. Frequented by hikers, mountaineers, cyclists and naturalists because it is an exceptional viewpoint towards the eastern and central massifs of the Picos de Europa. It is also known as Mirador del Oso because there is a monument here to the brown bear.

Right next to the road you can see an old blacksmith shop. This is where the surrounding residents shod their horses.

The blacksmith shop continues to be kept in good condition.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Camino Vadiniense and San Glorio Loop?
faqs.answer: The ideal time for cycling this route is typically from May to September. During winter months, particularly from November to April, high-altitude sections can be impassable due to snow, especially on the Camino Vadiniense.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse and challenging terrain. You'll encounter spectacular mountains, green valleys, and later, the Leonese plains. Surfaces vary significantly, including mountain-bike paths, paved rural roads, occasional main roads, herders' tracks, and even stretches of ancient Roman pavement. Be prepared for significant climbs, including a 1,200-meter ascent to the Pandetrave Pass and a sustained 6-7% gradient on the San Glorio Pass climb.

faqs.question: Is the Camino Vadiniense and San Glorio Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is not recommended for beginners. It is classified as difficult and physically demanding, requiring good off-road cycling skills and strong fitness due to significant elevation gains and varied, sometimes challenging, terrain.

faqs.question: What are the key highlights and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers numerous points of interest. You can experience the challenging Peña Oviedo Trail and the scenic Trail from Fuente Dé to Portilla de la Reina. Don't miss the Collado de Llesba – Bear Viewpoint for magnificent scenery and a chance to spot wildlife. The Old Blacksmith's Forge also offers a glimpse into local history.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or permits needed for cycling in Picos de Europa National Park?
faqs.answer: While there are no general entrance fees for Picos de Europa National Park, certain activities or areas might require specific permits, especially for protected zones or during peak seasons. For cycling, it's always advisable to check local regulations regarding trail access and any potential restrictions, particularly for off-road sections. Always respect park rules to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trail?
faqs.answer: The route is a loop starting and ending near Camaleño. Potes is a common starting point for sections of both the Camino Vadiniense and the San Glorio Pass. Parking is generally available in towns like Potes, but it's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends or during peak season, as spaces can fill up. Public transport options are limited, so planning your start and end points with parking in mind is crucial.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many trails in the Picos de Europa National Park, they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and livestock. Given the challenging terrain and potential for encounters with other animals, ensure your dog is fit for long distances and varied surfaces. Always carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

faqs.question: What should I know about waymarking and navigation on this route?
faqs.answer: Waymarking, particularly on sections of the Camino Vadiniense, can be vague. It is highly recommended to have good map-reading skills and to carry a reliable GPS device with the route loaded. This will help you navigate challenging segments and ensure you stay on track, especially in high-altitude areas where paths might be less defined.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates significant sections of the official Camino Vadiniense (approximately 45% of the route) and also includes parts of the Transcantábrica and Ruta de la Reconquista (Etapa 4). The San Glorio Pass is also a regular feature in the Vuelta a España.

faqs.question: Are there accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: Accommodation can be found in towns and villages along the route, such as Potes. However, outside of dedicated pilgrim hostels, prices may be higher as it passes through popular tourist areas. It's advisable to book accommodation in advance, especially during peak cycling season (May-September), as options can be limited in some remote sections.

faqs.question: What is a local secret or strategy for tackling the San Glorio Pass?
faqs.answer: While the San Glorio Pass climb is challenging, an insider tip is to consider the optional extra two to three-kilometer climb to Mirador de Llesba or Mirador Del Oso. This detour offers even more spectacular views and a higher vantage point, making the effort worthwhile for those seeking an additional reward. Be prepared for potentially light traffic, which can increase on weekends.

waypoints.name: Trail from Fuente Dé to Portilla de la Reina
